title: Retro-orbital injection of AAV in mice

materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"i4Yva","text":"MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4uOVE","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"PfzAH","text":"STEP MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"DD8QY","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"5ua5b","text":"It is important that the needel used has low friction so that the injection is at a slow and consistent rate.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":204769,"guid":"41ADED31341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"29G x 12.7mm 0.3ml syringe","mol_weight":0,"sku":"09230","url":"https://www.adwdiabetes.com/product/5741/ulticare-u100-pet-syringes-29g-0-3cc-12mm-29g","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":783,"name":"Ulticare","link":"http://www.ulticare.com/","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"1":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":204769,"guid":"41ADED31341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"29G x 12.7mm 0.3ml syringe","mol_weight":0,"sku":"09230","url":"https://www.adwdiabetes.com/product/5741/ulticare-u100-pet-syringes-29g-0-3cc-12mm-29g","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":783,"name":"Ulticare","link":"http://www.ulticare.com/","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Prepare virus dilution with sterile DPBS. Injection is 100 μL per mouse.
Take one mouse from the cage into an induction chamber and start isoflurane at 3-5% with flow rate of 0.8-1L/min
After breathing rate of the mouse has slowed (~1/s), indicating sufficient anesthetic depth, you can begin working with the mouse
Expose the eye ball with two fingers (above and below) and make sure the you are not pressing on the trachea and the mouse's breathing is regular.

Use a 31 G insulin syringe at a 30-degree angle beveled downwards until you hit the bone, retract a bit and slowly inject. See cited paper for more details. U-100 BD Ultra-Fine™ Short Insulin Syringes (Syringe and neecdle, BD Medical, BD328438, https://us.vwr.com/store/catalog/product.jsp?catalog_number=BD328438 )
Remove the needle slowly and press the eyelid shut for a few seconds.
Prior to recovery, apply 1-2 drops 0.5% ophthalmic Proparacaine to the injected eye
creator: Chelsie Steele
